title: Reagan and conservatism
description: Test your skills on the rise of debates about the role of government in the 1980s!

assessment_items.question_content:
Read the excerpt and answer the following question.

> “By reducing federal tax rates where they discourage individual initiative—especially personal income taxes—we can restore incentives, invite greater economic growth and at the same time help give us better government instead of bigger government.”

>-Source: Ronald Reagan, at his announcement for Presidential Candidacy, 1979

**The ideas expressed in the excerpt emerged most directly from a larger intellectual debate over the:**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: role of the government to solve social and economic problems.
separation of church and state in the development of laws.
power of business leaders to influence federal policy goals.
rise in military spending to deter the Soviet Union from attacking.

assessment_items.question_content:
Read the excerpt and answer the following question.

> “By reducing federal tax rates where they discourage individual initiative—especially personal income taxes—we can restore incentives, invite greater economic growth and at the same time help give us better government instead of bigger government.”

>-Source: Ronald Reagan, at his announcement for Presidential Candidacy, 1979

**The ideas expressed in the excerpt contributed most directly to which of the following?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the enactment of significant tax cuts for individuals
the creation of state taxes to reduce federal spending
the start of a period of stagflation, followed by a recession
the increase in the federal government’s size and scope

assessment_items.question_content:
Read the excerpt and answer the following question.

> “By reducing federal tax rates where they discourage individual initiative—especially personal income taxes—we can restore incentives, invite greater economic growth and at the same time help give us better government instead of bigger government.”

>-Source: Ronald Reagan, at his announcement for Presidential Candidacy, 1979

**Reagan’s account in the excerpt above most directly encouraged which of the following changes in subsequent years?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the deregulation of several industries
a cut in military and education spending
the suspension of local governments
an increase in taxes for businesses

assessment_items.question_content:
Read the excerpt and answer the following question.

> “We’re not cutting the budget simply for the sake of sounder financial management. This is only a first step toward returning power to the states and communities, only a first step toward reordering the relationship between citizenship and government. We can make government again responsive to people not only by cutting its size and scope and thereby ensuring that its legitimate functions are performed efficiently and justly.”

> -Source: President Ronald Reagan, in a speech to the Conservative Political Action Conference, 1981 

**Which of the following groups would be most likely to support the views expressed in the excerpt?**


[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: elderly men in the Sunbelt
black women in the Bible belt
Latino men in the Midwest
Asian women in the Northeast

assessment_items.question_content:
Read the excerpt and answer the following question.

> “We’re not cutting the budget simply for the sake of sounder financial management. This is only a first step toward returning power to the states and communities, only a first step toward reordering the relationship between citizenship and government. We can make government again responsive to people not only by cutting its size and scope and thereby ensuring that its legitimate functions are performed efficiently and justly.”

> -Source: President Ronald Reagan, in a speech to the Conservative Political Action Conference, 1981 

**The policies described in the excerpt contributed most directly to debates in the United States about the:**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: end of government social programs like Medicare.
president’s power to influence the federal budget.
hiring process for civil servants in the federal government.
financial support given to foreign nations resisting communism.

assessment_items.question_content:
Read the excerpt and answer the following question.

> “We’re not cutting the budget simply for the sake of sounder financial management. This is only a first step toward returning power to the states and communities, only a first step toward reordering the relationship between citizenship and government. We can make government again responsive to people not only by cutting its size and scope and thereby ensuring that its legitimate functions are performed efficiently and justly.”

> -Source: President Ronald Reagan, in a speech to the Conservative Political Action Conference, 1981 

**The ideas about the size of government expressed here are most similar to ideas about the proper size of government in what other period?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the period from after World War I through the 1920s
the period from after the Spanish-American War through the 1900s
the period from after the Korean War through the 1960s
the period from after the Stock Market Crash of 1929 through the 1930s

assessment_items.question_content:
> “It is no accident that this unmatched potential for progress and prosperity exists in three countries with such long-standing heritages of free government. A developing closeness among Canada, Mexico, and the United States–a North American accord–would permit achievement of that potential in each country beyond that which I believe any of them–strong as they are–could accomplish in the absence of such cooperation.”

>-Source: Ronald Reagan, at his announcement for Presidential Candidacy, 1979

**The ideas in the excerpt most directly contributed to which of the following?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the development of a free trade agreement between Canada, Mexico, and the United States
the reduction in citizenship requirements for undocumented immigrants from Mexico and Canada
the establishment of the North Atlantic Treaty Organization to protect the interests of North America
the enactment of tariffs on all foreign goods to support the production of American industries

assessment_items.question_content:
> “It is no accident that this unmatched potential for progress and prosperity exists in three countries with such long-standing heritages of free government. A developing closeness among Canada, Mexico, and the United States–a North American accord–would permit achievement of that potential in each country beyond that which I believe any of them–strong as they are–could accomplish in the absence of such cooperation.”

>-Source: Ronald Reagan, at his announcement for Presidential Candidacy, 1979

**The excerpt is best understood as a response to which of the following historical developments?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the globalization of the American economy
the spread of communism to South America
the rise in Canadian and Mexican immigration
the establishment of the North Atlantic Treaty Organization

assessment_items.question_content:
> “It is no accident that this unmatched potential for progress and prosperity exists in three countries with such long-standing heritages of free government. A developing closeness among Canada, Mexico, and the United States–a North American accord–would permit achievement of that potential in each country beyond that which I believe any of them–strong as they are–could accomplish in the absence of such cooperation.”

>-Source: Ronald Reagan, at his announcement for Presidential Candidacy, 1979

**The excerpt is best understood as a response to which of the following historical developments?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the creation of free trade agreements with several countries to spur economic growth
the establishment of an international alliance to ensure the mutual defense of member states
the reduction in citizenship requirements for people emigrating from Canada and Mexico
the rise in US efforts to replace the Mexican government with a constitutional democracy

assessment_items.question_content:
**Source 1:**

> “But when all we do to make a citizen is to say, ‘If you have been here illegally for a certain period of time, we will grandfather you in, we will give you amnesty,’ that does not establish a very high standard for the integrity of this precious right of citizenship.” 

> -Source: Senator William Armstrong (R-CO), on the Immigration Control and Reform Act of 1986, *Congressional Record,* 1986

** **

**Source 2:**

> “I also believe legalization is essential in order to regularize the status of those aliens who have built up equities in this country and who have contributed for years toward our economic and social well-being. . . . I wish to emphasize that with a 1982 date, we are talking about aliens who have been in this country almost five years — who have roots and families here—and who should be made a part of American society.” 

>-Source: Representative Peter Rodino (D-NJ), on the Immigration Control and Reform Act of 1986, *Congressional Record,* 1986

**In 1986, Congressman Rodino disagreed with Senator Armstrong in that Rodino believed that:**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the government should ease the path to citizenship for people who have lived in the United States for an extended period.
the government should not extend citizenship to any person who entered the United States without documentation.
the government should end all citizenship requirements to increase immigration to meet labor needs.
the government should only grant citizenship to immigrants who have had children on US soil.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Source 1:**

> “But when all we do to make a citizen is to say, ‘If you have been here illegally for a certain period of time, we will grandfather you in, we will give you amnesty,’ that does not establish a very high standard for the integrity of this precious right of citizenship.” 

> -Source: Senator William Armstrong (R-CO), on the Immigration Control and Reform Act of 1986, *Congressional Record,* 1986

** **

**Source 2:**

> “I also believe legalization is essential in order to regularize the status of those aliens who have built up equities in this country and who have contributed for years toward our economic and social well-being. . . . I wish to emphasize that with a 1982 date, we are talking about aliens who have been in this country almost five years — who have roots and families here—and who should be made a part of American society.” 

>-Source: Representative Peter Rodino (D-NJ), on the Immigration Control and Reform Act of 1986, *Congressional Record,* 1986

**The authors’ remarks in the two excerpts most directly reflected which of the following developments during the late twentieth century?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the increase in intense political debates over immigration policy
the institution of racial quotas to limit emigration from South America
the rise in fear that Soviet spies were immigrating to the United States
the Supreme Court’s decision to change citizenship requirements

assessment_items.question_content:
**Source 1:**

> “But when all we do to make a citizen is to say, ‘If you have been here illegally for a certain period of time, we will grandfather you in, we will give you amnesty,’ that does not establish a very high standard for the integrity of this precious right of citizenship.” 

> -Source: Senator William Armstrong (R-CO), on the Immigration Control and Reform Act of 1986, *Congressional Record,* 1986

** **

**Source 2:**

> “I also believe legalization is essential in order to regularize the status of those aliens who have built up equities in this country and who have contributed for years toward our economic and social well-being. . . . I wish to emphasize that with a 1982 date, we are talking about aliens who have been in this country almost five years — who have roots and families here—and who should be made a part of American society.” 

>-Source: Representative Peter Rodino (D-NJ), on the Immigration Control and Reform Act of 1986, *Congressional Record,* 1986

**Debates over the Immigration Control and Reform Act of 1986 in the excerpts most directly contributed to which of the following later characteristics of the United States government?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the enforcement of strict immigration laws and the deportation of undocumented immigrants
the establishment of immigration quotas, which limited emigration from Central and South America
the passage of the Patriot Act, which reduced restrictions for immigrants who served in the US military
the rise in fear about the communist threat that immigrants from Eastern Europe posed to the United States
